heading: Configure Your Backup Policy
body:
Each row in the **Backup Policy Frequency and Retention** table
represents a backup policy item. Configure the policy items and,
optionally, add new policy items to configure a new backup policy. See
the [retention considerations](snapshot-retention) before
finalizing your backup policy.

> **Tip:** Atlas displays the estimated number of snapshots
> associated with your changes below the
> **Backup Policy Frequency and Retention** table.

To specify a backup policy item using the Atlas UI:

Select the frequency unit from Frequency Unit
for an existing policy item.

Alternatively, click Add Frequency Unit to add a new
policy item to your backup policy. You can't specify multiple
Hourly or Daily backup policy items.

IMPORTANT: If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), only MongoDB support can delete
[Hourly Snapshot Retention Time](creating-backup-policy) specified in the
Backup Compliance Policy. To delete policy items specified in the Backup Compliance Policy, you
must [MongoDB Support](request-support) and complete an
extensive verification process.

Select the specific value for the frequency unit from
Every.

Specify the retention time for the policy item in
Retention Time and the units for the retention time
from the list the right. To learn more, see
[retention considerations](snapshot-retention).

At the bottom of the page, click Review
Changes to see a summary of differences between your existing
backup policy and your new backup policy.

(Optional) To apply the retention changes in the updated
backup policy to snapshots that Atlas took previously, check
Update retention time of existing
snapshots. You must check this **before** saving your changes.

IMPORTANT: This option affects only snapshots created by
the updated policy items and whose retention has not been
updated individually with the [Update Cloud
Backup Schedule for One Cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupclusterbackupschedule) API call.

(Optional) To delete snapshots and snapshot copies
associated with policies that you delete, check
Delete all snapshots and any associated snapshot
copies from removed policies. You must check this **before**
saving your changes.

Type `delete snapshots` in the prompt that appears when you
check this option.

Click
Confirm.
